title: ZXBrick Power-Ups
description: A practical guide to ZXBrick power-up examples, when to use them, and how they change pacing, risk, and scoring opportunities during gameplay.
summary: Power-ups are designed to alter risk and reward loops in short, replay-friendly intervals.

sections.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Power-ups are support systems that can alter risk windows and pace within level sessions.
They are available at the game-design level and are intended to reinforce replay habits, not replace basic movement control discipline.

sections.heading: Use power-ups with intent
paragraphs: Avoid using power-ups as a substitute for good movement discipline. Build one repeatable rhythm, then incorporate the available boosts deliberately.
If a boost changes ball timing unexpectedly, reset and start a short controlled set.
bullets: Pick a single variable and keep it stable for one replay cycle
Use shorter sessions when testing a new power-up
Record one thing that improved or worsened performance

faq.question: Do power-ups affect progression?
faq.answer: Power-ups are intended as gameplay tools within sessions and are part of the replay planning loop, alongside level progression and achievements.
